commit.message:
eng: remove debug console output for production cleanliness

Remove console.log/warn from templateResolver (fires on every config lookup),
writeBrainFact audit stub, useSubscription provisioning logs,
AdminTestOnboardingPage industry load log, createDefaultWorkflows success log.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): 429 rate limit backoff + admin_settings RLS simplification

1. Supabase client now uses custom fetch with automatic retry + exponential
   backoff for 429 responses. Prevents the auth death spiral where rate-limited
   token refresh fires SIGNED_OUT → clears session → retry → 429 again.

2. Simplified admin_settings RLS from has_role() dependency to simple
   user_id = auth.uid(). The has_role() check created a circular dependency
   with user_roles RLS, causing 42501 on INSERT for super_admin users.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

eng: fix admin_settings RLS INSERT policy (42501)

Split single FOR ALL policy into separate SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
policies. The combined policy was causing 42501 insufficient_privilege
on INSERT for super_admin users. Migration deployed via API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add shared SiteNav and update Groundtrust sub-nav

- components/SiteNav.tsx — new shared site nav with correct links: Services (/#services), Groundtrust (/groundtrust), Patterns (/patterns), About (/about), Contact (/#contact)
- app/page.tsx — replaced inline nav with <SiteNav />
- app/patterns/layout.tsx — replaced inline nav with <SiteNav />
- components/Nav.tsx — scoped to Groundtrust sub-nav only: How It Works, For Stewards, For Funders, For Land Trusts (removed About)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ARR should reflect total gross revenue over trailing 12 months,
not net (which is after store commissions).

Handle 202 responses gracefully in frontend signals and predictions

When worker hasn't finished processing, API returns 202 with job_id.
Frontend now retries once after a delay instead of crashing on
undefined properties. Shows friendly toast if still pending.

fix: coplanar detection for curved (circular) face boundaries

Update truck submodule: face_boundary_vertices() now uses
dense_sample_wire_points() to handle circular edges that have only 1
topological vertex. Fixes silent coplanar detection skip for cylinder
caps, circular pockets, and similar curved boundaries.

3 of 10 coplanar_curved tests now pass (cpb1, cpc4, cpu2).
Remaining 7 fail on deeper boolean issues, not coplanar detection.

fix: retry on 502/503/500 from Gemini, not just 429

502 Bad Gateway from Gemini means their backend is momentarily
overloaded — treat it as transient and retry with backoff like 429.
Also handles 503 and 500 the same way.

commit.message:
Auto-fix MOTD display issues in welcome command

The welcome command now automatically detects and fixes common Ubuntu
issues that prevent the welcome screen from showing on SSH login:
- Detects root vs non-root and uses sudo when needed
- Uncomments pam_motd.so lines in /etc/pam.d/sshd
- Sets PrintMotd to yes in /etc/ssh/sshd_config
- Restarts SSH service after config changes

Bumps version to 0.6.0.

commit.message:
Copilot/sub pr 10 (#20)

* fix: complete 4-lane remediation — tests, governance, security, CORS

Lane 1 (Testing): Add vitest infrastructure with MCP test cases
  covering JSON-RPC protocol, tool success/error paths, and defensive
  parsing.

Lane 2 (Governance): Add GitHub Actions CI/CD (ci.yml, deploy-worker,
  governance gates), org governance scripts (audit, enforce, remediate),
  ISSUE_TEMPLATE, release.yml, and governance-baseline templates.

Lane 3 (Security): Add .gitleaks.toml for secret scanning, security
  docs (access broker runbook, secret rotation checklist, scan report),
  .gitignore hardening.

Lane 4 (Code + UI): CORS OPTIONS + credentials support, MCP server
  expanded from 6 to 28 tools across 8 domains, integrations and
  validators updates, UI disputes widget and API client fixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- package.json: remove trailing space on kv:seed script line
- ci.yml: add npm test step, fix secret allowlist to catch bracket
  notation (secrets['NAME']) in addition to dot notation

- chittycompliance-dispatch.sh: replace string interpolation with
  jq -nc for all JSON payloads to prevent injection

  missingFiles and missingTriggers jq expressions
- connect.ts: proper AuthVariables typing instead of @ts-expect-error

- wrangler.toml: default PLAID_ENV to sandbox, production override
  in [env.production.vars]
- org-governance-pr-integration-loop.sh: add author verification
  against governance automation allowlist before auto-approve
- .gitignore: exclude timestamped governance report artifacts

commit.message:
Marlin N-padding: fix pre-repack padding for Qwen3-Next TP=2

The Marlin GPTQ kernel requires size_n % 64 == 0. Qwen3-Next's
linear_attn.in_proj_ba has output_size_per_partition=32 at TP=2.

Key fix: pad raw weights BEFORE gptq_marlin_repack (not after),
since the C++ repack function itself checks size_n % 64 == 0.
- qweight [K/pack, N]: pad dim=1 from 32 to 64
- scales [groups, N]: pad dim=1 from 32 to 64
- apply_weights: pass padded_n=64 to kernel, slice output to 32

Results: 26.3 tok/s (enforce-eager), 94% math accuracy.
torch.compile has separate issue with Mamba+Attention architecture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: robusztus main.c hardware watchdog + reconnection state machine

- Hardware watchdog (wdt0, 30s timeout, WDT_FLAG_RESET_SOC) — robot crash esetén auto reboot
- DTR timeout (3s max) — autonóm működés USB monitor nélkül
- 4-fázisú reconnection loop: ping → session_init → run → cleanup
- ros_session_init() / ros_session_fini() clean micro-ROS lifecycle
- watchdog_feed() minden fázisban hívva (soha nem fagylal)
- channel_manager_destroy_entities() hozzáadva session cleanup-hoz
- Ethernet link UP wait + DHCP timeout robustness

commit.message:
fix: use relative API URL default so frontend works through Caddy proxy

The next.config.js env section was baking http://localhost:8000/api/v1
into the JS bundle at build time, causing "failed to fetch" errors
in production. Changed default to /api/v1 so requests go through
the Caddy reverse proxy.

commit.message:
feat(isochrone): add large-response handling with temporary resources (#131)

* Add server-level icons with light and dark theme support

Implements MCP server icons at the correct architectural level (server
initialization) instead of at the tool level. Adds both light and dark
theme variants of the Mapbox logo using base64-encoded SVG data URIs.

- Add mapbox-logo-black.svg for light theme backgrounds
- Add mapbox-logo-white.svg for dark theme backgrounds
- Update server initialization to include icons array with theme property
- Use 800x180 SVG logos embedded as base64 data URIs

This replaces the previous incorrect approach of adding icons to
individual tools, which was not aligned with the MCP specification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update @modelcontextprotocol/sdk to 1.25.2

Updates the MCP SDK from 1.25.1 to 1.25.2 and recreates the output
validation patch for the new version. The patch continues to convert
strict output schema validation errors to warnings, allowing tools
to gracefully handle schema mismatches.

Changes:
- Update @modelcontextprotocol/sdk from ^1.25.1 to ^1.25.2
- Recreate SDK patch for version 1.25.2
- Remove obsolete 1.25.1 patch file
- All 397 tests pass with new SDK version

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(isochrone): add large-response handling with temporary resources

Isochrone responses for complex multi-contour requests can exceed the
50KB context limit. Add the same size-threshold pattern used by
DirectionsTool: when the response is large, store the full GeoJSON in
temporaryResourceManager and return a summary with the resource URI.

Clients can retrieve the full data via readResource('mapbox://temp/isochrone-<id>').

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve duplicate runner naming race condition and add bulk creation (#17) (#45)

Fix the race condition in duplicateRunner() where rapid duplicate clicks
generate the same name because addRunner() hasn't appended the new runner
yet. Names are now reserved in a pendingRunnerNames set before async work
begins. Also strips trailing -N suffixes from base names so duplicating
"runner-2" yields "runner-3" instead of "runner-2-2".

Adds bulk runner creation: the Add Runner form now includes an instance
count stepper (1-20) with a live name preview, and addRunners() handles
sequential registration with upfront name reservation and progress
reporting.

Closes #17

Co-authored-by: OmniClaw Agent <omniclaw@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(a11y): resolve all accessibility violations found in audit

- Modal: add tabIndex={-1} to dialog panel (correct focus management) and
  suppress jsx-a11y false-positive for keyboard handler on role="dialog"
  (WAI-ARIA requires keyboard interaction on modals)
- Radio: remove aria-invalid from input[type="radio"] — not supported on
  the radio role per ARIA spec; error state is conveyed at FormField level
- Typography: fix colorMuted color contrast — replace opacity:0.6 on
  #1e1e24 (rendered #78787c, 4.39:1) with var(--color-text-muted)
  (#56565c, ~7.3:1), meeting WCAG AA 4.5:1 requirement

All 17 components pass axe-core WCAG 2.2 AA runtime scan (0 violations).
Static jsx-a11y scan is now clean (0 errors).

Add werk log command for project activity timeline

Unified reverse-chronological feed of high-signal events: task status
changes, decisions, and session summaries. Default 20 entries, one line
each. Use --verbose for notes/rationale/details, -n to control limit.

Gives agents a single command to orient on project history without
reading individual audit trails or session records.
